stories.title: Burruchaga Stuns Cobolli at Umag — First Top-10 Win of Career

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Roman Andres Burruchaga — No. 67 — just beat top seed Cobolli 6-2, 6-4 at Umag.

First Top-10 win of his career. ATP Tour: "one of the best matches of the year."

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Umag

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Burruchaga at Umag: 78% first-serve pts, 4/7 break points converted.

Cobolli's first match since Wimbledon — gone in two sets. Argentine qualifier heads to his first ATP QF.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #CroatiaOpen

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:30 AM ET

Roman Andres Burruchaga walked into Umag as No. 67 in the world. He walked out of his second-round match as a player who had just beaten a top-10 seed for the first time in his career.

The Argentine defeated top seed Flavio Cobolli 6-2, 6-4 on Wednesday — the ATP Tour called it "one of the best matches of the year." Cobolli hadn't played since reaching the Wimbledon quarterfinals, and Burruchaga made him pay for any rust, winning 78% of his first-serve points and converting four of seven break points.

What makes it remarkable: all 13 of Burruchaga's tour-level wins have come on clay. He's carved out an identity as a clay specialist, and Umag is his stage.

Now he heads to his first ATP quarterfinal. The draw opens up without the No. 1 seed, and Davidovich Fokina, Arnaldi, and Droguet are the other names standing between the Argentine qualifier and what could be the biggest week of his tennis life.

Which clay-court upsets have you seen that really shocked you?

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 10:30 AM ET

Roman Andres Burruchaga — No. 67 in the world, undefeated in Umag R2. First career Top-10 win. First career ATP quarterfinal. All 13 of his wins on clay. The Argentine is writing something special in Croatia this week.

What do you think — can he go all the way?

article.headline: Burruchaga Stuns Cobolli at Umag for First Career Top-10 Win

stories.title: Alcaraz Appears on Cincinnati Entry List — Return Imminent After Three Months Out

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Carlos Alcaraz is officially on the Cincinnati Open entry list.

His wrist is "completely healed." After missing RG, Wimbledon, and Canada, the 7-time Grand Slam champion is targeting a return in August.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Alcaraz

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Alcaraz in Cincinnati. Alcaraz at the US Open.

He's the defending champion at both. After 3 months out, the No. 3 in the world is edging back toward the hard-court swing.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Alcaraz

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

The tennis world has been holding its breath for three months. Carlos Alcaraz is finally ready to breathe again.

The seven-time Grand Slam champion has appeared on the Cincinnati Open entry list, and reports confirm his right wrist is "completely healed" after the tenosynovitis he suffered on April 14 in Barcelona that ended his spring season before it really began. He missed Roland Garros. He missed Wimbledon. He missed the Canadian Open. But Cincinnati — scheduled August 13-23 — is now the target, with his team saying the next two weeks of high-intensity training will determine when they formally confirm his return.

What awaits him: he is the defending champion in Cincinnati AND the defending champion at the US Open (August 31 – September 13). Alcaraz has a lot of points to defend, and a lot of momentum to rebuild. The good news is that his body appears to have cooperated.

The North American hard-court swing is shaping up to be one for the ages. Sinner is fresh off back-to-back Wimbledon titles. Zverev is a Roland Garros champion. And now Alcaraz — ranked No. 3 — appears to be back.

How excited are you to see Alcaraz return? Will he be match-sharp in time for the US Open?

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 3:00 PM ET

Carlos Alcaraz — on the Cincinnati entry list, wrist healed, return imminent. After three months away from the Tour, the defending champion at Cincinnati AND the US Open is coming back. The North American swing just got a lot more interesting.

Are you surprised he's targeting Cincinnati instead of waiting until the US Open?

article.headline: Alcaraz Targets Cincinnati Return After Wrist Cleared — US Open Defense Now Back On

stories.title: Bastad Sets QF Lineup — Borges Beats Former Finalist Dimitrov; Vallejo Makes History

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Adolfo Daniel Vallejo — the first Paraguayan to reach an ATP QF since 2006.

Nuno Borges also through after topping Dimitrov 6-4, 6-2 at Bastad.

Former Bastad champion. Back in the quarterfinals.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Bastad

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 PM ET

The Nordea Open in Bastad served up some memorable moments on Wednesday — and one piece of history no one saw coming.

Adolfo Daniel Vallejo, a Paraguayan qualifier, defeated Botic van de Zandschulp 4-6, 6-3, 7-6(4) in nearly three hours to become the first player from his country to reach a tour-level quarterfinal since Ramon Delgado in Mumbai all the way back in 2006. Two hours and 57 minutes of clay-court warfare for a moment that spans two decades of national tennis history.

Elsewhere, Nuno Borges — who famously won Bastad in 2024 — is right back in the quarterfinals after beating Grigor Dimitrov 6-4, 6-2. Stefano Travaglia added to the drama by knocking out No. 4 seed Mariano Navone 6-4, 6-2.

Meanwhile in Iasi, Yulia Putintseva, Tamara Zidansek, Clara Burel, and Petra Marcinko all advanced to their quarterfinals on Wednesday.

The clay swing is barely a week old and already delivering upsets, history, and drama.

Which of these clay-court results surprised you most?

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 9:00 PM ET

Vallejo of Paraguay in his first ATP quarterfinal. Borges — who won Bastad in 2024 — back in the last 8. Navone [4] gone. Wednesday at Bastad served up history and surprises in equal measure.

Who are you backing to take the Nordea Open title?

article.headline: Bastad Quarterfinals Set: Vallejo Makes Paraguay History, Borges Back in Last 8

stories.title: WTA Athens R2 Complete — Sakkari Dominant, Parks Survives Thriller; QFs Friday

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Maria Sakkari has dropped 3 games across 2 Athens matches.

6-0, 7-6(1) in R1. 6-1, 6-2 today. On home clay. In front of her home crowd.

QF Friday: Alycia Parks (2-0 H2H) awaits.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Athens

facebook.long_form:
Window: 6:00 PM ET

Maria Sakkari is playing some of the best tennis of her 2026 season — and she's doing it at home.

The Greek No. 4 seed dropped just 3 games against Harriet Dart on Wednesday at the Athens Olympic Tennis Centre, winning 6-1, 6-2 in front of her home crowd. In two Athens matches she has dropped just 3 games total. The crowd is electric; the tennis is even better.

The quarterfinals on Friday will test her — Alycia Parks, who holds a 2-0 head-to-head record over Sakkari, awaits. The American survived a thriller in the second round, saving a set and rallying from 6-7(4), 6-2, 7-5 against Mai Hontama. Qinwen Zheng also continues to roll, while Sara Bejlek posted a dominant 6-2, 6-2 to reach the last 8.

Friday is going to be special. First WTA event in Athens in 35 years, and the home favourite is delivering.

Would you back Sakkari to overcome her H2H deficit against Parks on home clay?

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 6:00 PM ET

Maria Sakkari in Athens — 3 games dropped across 2 matches. On home clay, in front of her home crowd, she looks unstoppable. But Parks holds a 2-0 H2H lead ahead of Friday's quarterfinal. Something has to give.

Who wins — Sakkari or Parks?

article.headline: WTA Athens R2: Sakkari Rolls at Home, Parks Survives Thriller; Quarterfinals Set for Friday

stories.title: Gstaad Day 4 — Bublik and Ruud Finally Enter, Tsitsipas Faces Swiss Wild Card

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Gstaad Day 4.

Bublik (defending champion) vs Halys. Ruud (former champion) vs Faria.

Two headliners. First matches of the week. Swiss clay finally heating up.

#Tennis #TennisFanrecap #Gstaad

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

The EFG Swiss Open Gstaad has been running four days and two of its biggest names are only just getting started.

Defending champion Alexander Bublik and two-time former winner Casper Ruud have both had first-round byes, but Thursday is the day they finally take to the mountain-air clay courts of Gstaad. Bublik — who won the title in 2025 — faces Quentin Halys. Ruud, who won Gstaad twice before, takes on Jaime Faria, the qualifier who shocked Stan Wawrinka in his emotional homecoming on Monday.

Stefanos Tsitsipas is already into his next match, resuming against Swiss wild card Jerome Kym after his comfortable 6-4, 6-4 win over Ignacio Buse.

Gstaad is one of the most beautiful venues on the clay calendar — mountain backdrop, wood-paneled centre court, passionate Swiss crowd. Thursday is the day it properly comes alive.

What is it about Gstaad that makes it so special on the ATP clay schedule?

---
facebook.image_caption:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

Bublik (defending champion) vs Halys. Ruud (former champion) vs Faria. Tsitsipas vs Kym. Gstaad Day 4 has the names. The Swiss clay swing is entering its decisive stretch.

Who takes the title Sunday?

article.headline: Gstaad Day 4: Defending Champion Bublik and Former Winner Ruud Finally Take the Court
